An abnormal kidney function test which may indicate that your kidneys are not working properly. When the kidneys do not work properly, wastes can build up in your blood, leading to swelling in the arms and legs, tiredness and weakness. This could become severe, requiring hospitalization and dialysis to clean the wastes out of your blood. If the wastes are not removed from your blood, this could cause seizures and be life threatening.

Coronary heart disease (CHD) is a common form of heart diesase which can lead to heart failure. CHD causes a waxy substance called plaque to build up inside the arteries of the heart. These arteries supply oxygen-rich blood to your heart muscle making them difficult to function. Over time plaque build up in these arteries can harden or break apart reducing the flow of oxygen-rich blood to the heart. This may cause the formation of a blood clot, chest pain or a heart attack.

This is a condition in which immune cells from the donor   s tissue attack your organs. The risk of severe GVHD depends on the type of BMT (bone marrow transplant) or SCT (stem cell transplant) and the quality(how closely) the patient and donor cells match. There are acute and chronic forms of GVHD. Acute GVHD usually affects the skin, intestines, and liver and may start one week to three months after transplant. Chronic GVHD begins later and can affect these organs as well as the lung, mucous membranes and/or other organs. Both of these can require treatment and be serious or life threatening.

A disorder in which the body   s immune system attacks part of the peripheral nervous system. Symptoms of this disorder include varying degrees of weakness or tingling sensations in the legs. In many instances, the weakness and abnormal sensations spread to the arms and upper body. These symptoms can increase in intensity until certain muscles cannot be used at all and, when severe, the patient is almost totally paralyzed. In these cases, the disorder is life threatening, potentially interfering with breathing and, at times, with blood pressure or heart rate.

The study treatment may cause an increased risk of infection, such as herpes meningoencephalitis. The layers of thin tissue that cover your brain are called meninges and an infection in these tissues is called meningitis. When your brain becomes inflamed or infected, the problem is called encephalitis. If both the meninges and the brain appear to be involved, the condition is called meningoencephalitis. Symptoms of herpes meningoencephalitis include headache, fever, neck stiffness, sensitivity to light, seizures, trouble thinking clearly, personality changes, unusual behavior and/or unconsciousness. This infection may become life-threatening and in severe cases cause long-term brain damage including trouble thinking, controlling movement, hearing, seeing, or speaking. This infection is usually treated with IV anti-viral medication but in severe cases long-term care and medications would be needed.

A neurologic deficit is a decrease in the function of the brain, spinal cord, muscles, and/or nerves. Neurologic deficits include inability to speak, decreased sensation, loss of balance, weakness, dificulty thinking, visual changes, abnormal reflexes, and problems walking.

A condition affecting the nerves supplying the arms and legs. Typically, the feet and hands are involved first. If sensory nerves are involved, numbness, tingling, and pain are felt, and if motor nerves are involved you will experience weakness.
Weakness or loss of muscle function caused by damage to nerves
Condition of the nervous system that causes numbness, tingling, burning.

Condition in which the number of white blood cells called neutrophils are abnormally low. This increases the risk of infection, which may be serious or life threatening.  Symptoms of infection may include fever, pain, redness, and/or difficulty breathing.

Decreased number of a specific type of white blood cells. This increases the risk of infection, which may be serious or life threatening.  Symptoms of infection may include fever, pain, redness, and/or difficulty breathing.
Irregular heartbeat

The study treatment may cause an increased risk of infection. An infection may occur anywhere and it may become life-threatening. A low number of white blood cells may increase the risk of infection.  Symptoms of infection may include fever, pain, redness, and/or difficulty breathing.
Night blindness
Rapid, jerky movements of the eye
Significantly more body fat than normal.
Damage to the surface of the eye
Double vision

The study treatment may cause an increased risk of an opportunistic infection (an infection caused by an organism that usually does not cause illness, but does so when the immune response or resistance to infection is impaired).  A low number of white blood cells (lymphopenia) may increase the risk of infection.  It may become life-threatening.  Symptoms of infection may include fever, pain, redness, and/or difficulty breathing.

An infection caused by an organism that usually does not cause illness, but does so when a person   s immune response (resistance) to infection is impaired. A low number of white blood cells may increase the risk of infection.  Symptoms of infection may include fever, pain, redness, and/or difficulty breathing.
Swelling of the nerve in the back of the eye which is responsible for vision

Fungal infection in the mouth  The study treatment may cause an increased risk of infection, such as oral thrush (a fungal infection in the mouth).  A low number of white blood cells may increase the risk of infection.  It may become life-threatening.  Symptoms of infection may include fever, pain, redness, and/or difficulty breathing.
Bone pain

The study treatment may cause an increased risk of infection, such as osteomyelitis (an infection of the bone).  A low number of white blood cells may increase the risk of infection.  It may become life-threatening.  Symptoms of infection may include fever, pain, redness, and/or difficulty breathing.
Rare bone disorder characterized by dense bone
Bone disorder characterized by loss of bone leading to increased risk of fracture

The study treatment may cause an increased risk of an outer ear infection.  A low number of white blood cells may increase the risk of infection.  It may become life-threatening.  Symptoms of infection may include fever, pain, redness, and/or difficulty breathing.

The study treatment may cause an increased risk of an middle ear infection.  A low number of white blood cells may increase the risk of infection.  It may become life-threatening.  Symptoms of infection may include fever, pain, redness, and/or difficulty breathing.
Inflammation of the ear canal
Fluid in the middle ear that can cause pain, general hearing loss, fever, irritability, headache, anorexia & vomiting.
Fluid-filled lump in the ovary
Pain
Redness, pain or peeling of palms and soles.
Fast and hard heartbeat
Decrease in enzymes from the pancreas that help digest food

Inflammation of the pancreas causing pain in the upper abdomen. This could become severe and cause nausea and vomiting, fever and rapid heart rate. This could require hospitalization and may be life threatening.

Abnormal decrease in the levels of all type of blood cells. This is associated with anemia, decreased platelets, and decreased white blood cells. This can lead to fatigue, increased risk of bleeding, and/or increased risk of infection. This may be serious or life threatening.
Abnormal reactions in the nose and sinuses
Irregular fast heartbeat

Collection of fluid around the heart which may cause a life threatening condition where the heart is unable to pump correctly due to the external pressure. This may require medical intervention to drain the fluid and remove the pressure.

Inflammation of the sac around the heart. May manifest as chest pain that varies with each breath. Pain often increases when lying down and decreases upon sitting up. Fever, cough, and palpitations are common as well. This may be serious and require medical intervention.
Gum disease
Disorder of arteries causing inadequate blood supply to extremities.

The study treatment may cause an increased risk of an infection around an ostomy site or surgical opening into the skin.  A low number of white blood cells may increase the risk of infection.  It may become life-threatening.  Symptoms of infection may include fever, pain, redness, and/or difficulty breathing.
Very small broken blood vessels in skin or lining of the mouth which may result in bleeding
Painful feeling of arm or leg that has been amputated
Irritation or inflammation of a vein
Lumps and clots in the lymph vessels that drain through lymph nodes
Increased sensitivity to sunlight
Impaired movement of the muscle between the chest and belly caused by damage to the nerve controlling that muscle.
Pink Urine
Decreased number of blood cells that help to clot blood. This is associated with an increased risk of bleeding.

The study treatment may cause an increased risk of an infection of the membranes that line the chest cavity and cover the lungs,  An infection may occur anywhere and it may become life-threatening. A low number of white blood cells may increase the risk of infection.  Symptoms of infection may include fever, pain, redness, and/or difficulty breathing.
Collection of fluid around the lungs in the chest cavity, which can cause shortness of breath and may require treatment.

Pneumocystis pneumonia is a lung infection that can occur in the setting of a suppressed immune system as a result of treatment with chemotherapy or steroids. This may cause shortness of breath and decreased oxygen levels and could be life threatening.

The study treatment may cause an increased risk of infection, such as pneumonia (a infection of the lungs).  A low number of white blood cells may increase the risk of infection.  It may become life-threatening.  Symptoms of infection may include fever, pain, redness, and/or difficulty breathing.
Inflammation of the lungs, which can cause shortness of breath and difficulty breathing. If severe, this can be life threatening.
Inflammation of the lungs, which can cause shortness of breath and difficulty breathing. If severe, this can be life threatening.
Collection of air outside the lungs in the space surrounding the lungs. May require lung re-inflation with a chest tube.
Problems with blood flow from the digestive tract, the spleen, pancreas, and gall-bladder to the liver
Chest pain
Loss of function of the ovaries that can lead to menopause
Inflammation of the lining of the rectum

A rare and severe viral infection of the brain, called PML which can cause brain damage, memory loss, trouble thinking, muscle weakness, blindness and death. Notify your study doctor immediately if you develop trouble thinking or walking, a decrease in strength in you arms or legs, or changes in your vision or hearing.  This is a serious and life-threatening infection.
Abnormal production of prolactin. Low levels of prolactin in females can result in the inability to produce breast milk.
Irregular protrusion of tissue through the opening of an ostomy
Abnormal electrical conduction within the heart which may lead to arrhythmias or irregular heartbeat.
When the eye sticks out further than normal position; sunken eyeball
Excess protein in the urine. This may cause fluid retention.
Itchy skin
Hallucinations; delusions
Early onset of puberty

A blood clot that causes a sudden blockage in a lung blood vessel, usually due to a blood clot that traveled to the lung from the leg. A pulmonary embolism is a serious condition that can cause: Permanent damage to part of your lung from lack of blood flow to lung tissue; Low oxygen levels in your blood; Damage to other organs in your body from not getting enough oxygen; If a clot is large, or if there are many clots, a pulmonary embolism can cause death.
Tissue in the lungs becomes stiff making breathing difficult, resulting in shortness of breath, and if severe, can cause heart failure.
Scarring of the lungs that can affect your ability to breath and may make you short of breath.  This may be serious or life threatening.

Abnormally high blood pressure in the blood vessels in the lungs, which makes it harder to pump blood into the lungs. May cause fatigue, shortness of breath, chest pain, and ankle swelling. Can lead to loss of consciousness and could be serious and life threatening.
Irregular heartbeat
Abnormal function of nerves in the brain and spine which control muscles of voluntary movement

Skin reaction that develops when chemotherapy is administered during or after radiation treatment, characterized by one or more of symptoms of redness, tenderness, swelling, peeling, pain and discoloration, that can be severe or disfiguring.
Acne; pimples

An autoimmune disorder causing blood vessels to spasm when exposed to cold. This occurs especially in the fingers and toes causing them to turn red, pale, and then blue in succession and is usually painful. If this becomes severe, it can progress to local tissue death.
Stunted growth

Kidney failure which is when both of your kidneys fail and your body holds fluid which can be serious or life threatening. Your blood pressure rises and harmful wastes build up in your body. You may experience fatigue, nausea, and loss of appetite. When this happens, you need treatment to replace the work of your failed kidneys such as dialysis.

Difficulty breathing with low levels of oxygen in the blood, which could be serious and life threatening and require you to have a tube inserted into your windpipe that is hooked up to a machine to help you breathe.
Restlessness, irritability
Decrease in heart's ability to pump blood. This may be serious or life threatening.
Separation of the retina from its connection at the back of the eye. May require surgical repair/treatment.

A group of signs and symptoms that may be caused by retinoic acid and may include fever, chest pain, shortness of breath, chest x- ray abnormalities, fluid buildup around the heart, and decreased ability of the lung to absorb oxygen.
Damage to the eye that decreases vision
A rare reversible risk of confusion, visual loss, seizures and coma that can happen in association with very high blood pressure.

Rhabdomyolysis is a breakdown of muscle fibers. It occurs when muscle cells die and release cell contents into the blood stream. It can cause muscle pain and a number of health problems, including damage to the kidneys. If severe, this could be life threatening.
Decrease in heart's ability to pump blood. This may be serious or life threatening.
Chills and shivering
Destruction of the white of the eye.
A second cancer related to the treatment of your first cancer.  This usually occurs years after treatment for the first cancer.
Convulsion or seizure

A serious condition that occurs in response to an infection that causes widespread inflammation, resulting in poor blood supply to vital organs (Sepsis). Symptoms may include a fast heart rate, fever, confusion and rapid breathing. Sepsis can rapidly lead to a life threatening clinical deterioration.

A serious condition that occurs in response to an infection that causes widespread inflammation, resulting in poor blood supply to vital organs (Sepsis). Symptoms may include a fast heart rate, fever, confusion and rapid breathing. Sepsis can rapidly lead to a life threatening clinical deterioration.

A serious condition that occurs in response to an infection that causes widespread inflammation, resulting in poor blood supply to vital organs (Sepsis). Symptoms may include a fast heart rate, fever, confusion and rapid breathing. Sepsis can rapidly lead to a life threatening clinical deterioration.
Abnormal fluid collection in a tissue or organ
Allergic reaction that causes fever, aches and pains in the joints, skin rash and swollen lymph glands.
When you are unable to maintain enough blood pressure to keep enough blood delivered to your organs. Shock is a life threatening condition that may require hospitalization.
Irregular heartbeat
Irregular heartbeat
Slow heart rate which can cause dizziness, fainting and tiredness.
Fast heartbeat

The study treatment may cause an increased risk of infection, such as a sinus infection.  An infection may occur anywhere and it may become life-threatening. A low number of white blood cells may increase the risk of infection.  Symptoms of infection may include fever, pain, redness, and/or difficulty breathing.
Allergic skin reaction
Death of tissue
Sleepiness
Speech problems
Abnormal curvature of the spine
Decreased spleen function (possible infection and/or blood abnormality)
Narrowing of a duct, tube, or one of the heart valves
Inability to have children

Alternate Name: SJS, Toxic Epidermal Necrolysis, erythema multiforme, Stevens Johnson Syndrome
Lay Language: Severe skin and digestive tract reaction that may include rash and sloughing or breakdown of tissue. This may manifest as various blisters, hives, and other lesions in various locations on the body including palms and soles, face & other extremities. This is serious and may be life threatening.

Loss of consciousness caused by a decreased flow of blood to the brain. Can be caused by passing interference to the normal output of the heart. May be serious and/or life threatening and require medical intervention.
Sores in the mouth and esophagus, which may be painful and cause difficulty swallowing.
Pink or purple stretch marks on the skin
Stunned state in which it is difficult to get a response or the attention of the subject
Sudden Death
Irregular heartbeat
Extra heartbeats
Irregular heartbeat
Swelling of the face, fingers and lower legs
Fainting
Enlargement of small blood vessels near the surface of the skin, causing redness of the skin or lining of the nose or mouth
Low number of platelets, which may cause bleeding and bruising. Bleeding may be serious or life threatening and may required a blood transfusion.
Formation or presence of a blood clot inside a blood vessel.

Abnormal clotting of blood in small blood vessels This is associated with decrease in platelets with risk of bleeding and decreased kidney function which may be severe enough to require dialysis. It may also be associated with changes in ability to think.

The study treatment may cause anxiety and irritability, insomnia, intolerance to heat, fever, weight loss despite increased appetite, muscular weakness, missed periods (amenorrhea), erectile dysfunction, and high fecal output.
Ringing in the ears
Irregular and rapid heartbeat that can be caused by some medications. May be life threatening and may require medical intervention.

Alternate Name: TEN, Lyell's syndrome, Stevens Johnson Syndrome, erythema multiforme
Lay Language: Toxic Epidermal Necrolysis (TEN) is a rare, life-threatening skin condition that is usually caused by a reaction to a drug. The top layer of skin detaches from the lower layers of skin all over the body. This is similar to the skin damage from a severe burn and is serious and life threatening.
A brief episode of decreased oxygen to the brain causing blurred vision, dizziness, faintness, and numbness.
Irregular heartbeat
Difficulty or limitation to mouth opening
Abnormal blood test (possible heart problems)
Sudden growth in tumor or worsening of tumor related problems.

Tumor lysis syndrome (TLS) is caused by the sudden, rapid death of cancer cells in response to treatment. When cancer cells die they may spill their inner (intracellular) contents, which accumulate faster then they can be eliminated. This debris from the cancer cells can change the balance of the chemistry of the body, which can be dangerous. Symptoms of tumor lysis syndrome include severe nausea and vomiting, shortness of breath, an irregular heartbeat, kidney failure, urine abnormalities, severe fatigue and/or joint pain.
Diabetes requiring insulin
Inflammation of a portion of the bowel which can be seious or life threatening
Irritation or destruction of skin or other body tissues.
Condition where the kidneys lose salts and can lead to a decrease in kidney function and a change in the acid and base balance of the blood.
Increased need to urinate
Difficulty emptying the bladder

A low number of white blood cells may increase the risk of infection.  Symptoms of infection may include fever, pain, redness, and/or difficulty breathing.
Swollen raised areas on the skin that are intensely itchy
Swollen raised areas on the skin that are intensely itchy
Inflammation of the iris of the eye
Irritation or sores of the lining of the vagina
Inflammation of the vagina
Abnormal heart valves.
Enlarged veins, usually in legs or lining of tube between mouth and stomach
Inflammation of blood vessels that can lead to damage of different organs
Blood vessel spasm (possible blockage of blood flow)
Sudden drop in blood pressure that may cause fainting

Alternate Name: VOD, Heptatic Veno-Occlusive Disease, veno-occlusive hepatic disease, Veno-Occlusive Liver Disease
Lay Language: Hepatic veno-occlusive disease is a condition that is characterized by damage to blood vessels in the liver and liver cells. Although it may be mild and not require further treatment, sometimes it may cause a severe decrease in liver function and may be life threatening or fatal.
Irregular heartbeat

Irregular heartbeat

Damage to a blood vessel that carries blood to the heart
Inflammation of the liver caused by a virus

Damage to a blood vessel in a belly organ
Decrease in blood flow to internal body organs
Decreased vision or vision changes that may be permanent.
Vision loss or vision changes that may be reversible.
Seeing flashing lights or floaters
Eyes are more sensitive than normal to light.
Decreased amount of air that a person can exhale (breath out)
Patches of skin color loss
Bleeding into the eyeball

Alternate Name: hoarseness, loss or alteration in voice, laryngitis
